title: Vehicle hvac system
abstract: A heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system for a vehicle. The HVAC system includes an airflow inlet case defining a fresh air inlet and a recirculation air inlet. The airflow inlet case includes an evaporator and a filter both aligned with an axis of rotation of a blower configured to draw airflow through the airflow inlet case into a heater case. The recirculation air inlet faces the heater case. The airflow inlet case is configured to be mounted on an engine side of a vehicle firewall such that the recirculation air inlet is at the firewall.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system for a vehicle, the HVAC system comprising: an airflow inlet case defining a fresh air inlet and a recirculation air inlet, the airflow inlet case including an evaporator and a filter both aligned with an axis of rotation of a blower configured to draw airflow through the airflow inlet case into a heater case, the recirculation air inlet faces the heater case; wherein the airflow inlet case is configured to be mounted on an engine side of a vehicle firewall with the recirculation air inlet at the firewall. 2. The HVAC system of claim 1 , wherein both the heater case and the blower are configured to be mounted on a passenger cabin side of the vehicle firewall. 3. The HVAC system of claim 1 , wherein an inlet face of the evaporator extends parallel to an inlet face of the filter. 4. The HVAC system of claim 1 , further comprising a blower case including the blower, the blower case is between, and coupled to, the airflow inlet case and the heater case. 5. The HVAC system of claim 4, wherein the blower case extends across the firewall when the HVAC system is mounted in the vehicle. 6. The HVAC system of claim 1 , wherein the heater case includes a heater aligned with the axis of rotation of the blower. 7. The HVAC system of claim 6, wherein a longitudinal axis extending through the axis of rotation of the blower extends through a center of the 

 evaporator, a center of the heater, and a center of the filter, the filter is on a side of the evaporator opposite to the heater case. 8. The HVAC system of claim 7, wherein the axis of rotation of the blower extends perpendicular to a face of the evaporator and a face of the filter. 9. The HVAC system of claim 2, wherein the recirculation air inlet overlaps the blower case. 10. A heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system for a vehicle, the HVAC system comprising: a blower case including a blower configured to rotate about an axis of rotation; a heater case on a first side of the blower case, the heater case including a heater aligned with the axis of rotation of the blower; and an airflow inlet case on a second side of the blower case, the airflow inlet case including an evaporator aligned with the axis of rotation of the blower, the airflow inlet case defining a fresh air inlet and a recirculation air inlet, the recirculation air inlet faces the heater case; wherein the airflow inlet case is configured to be mounted on an engine side of a vehicle firewall, and the heater case is configured to be mounted on a passenger cabin side of the vehicle firewall. 1 1. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the blower case is coupled to the heater case and the airflow inlet case. 12. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the blower case extends across the firewall when the HVAC system is mounted in the vehicle. 13. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein a longitudinal axis extending along the axis of rotation of the blower extends through a center of the evaporator, a center of the heater, and a center of a filter that is on a side of the evaporator opposite to the heater case. 14. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the axis of rotation of the blower extends perpendicular to a face of the evaporator and a face of a filter that is on a side of the evaporator opposite to the heater case. 15. The HVAC system of claim 14, wherein the face of the evaporator and the face of the filter extend parallel to each other. 16. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the recirculation air inlet is at the firewall when the HVAC system is installed in the vehicle. 17. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the recirculation air inlet faces the blower. 18. The HVAC system of claim 10, wherein the recirculation air inlet is at an end of a recirculation air duct of the airflow inlet case, the recirculation air duct is monolithic with at least a portion of the airflow inlet case. 19. The HVAC system of claim 18, wherein the recirculation air inlet is at an end of a recirculation air duct of the airflow inlet case, the recirculation air duct extends over the blower case.
